column1: # Shape validation — defines the SOURCE design pattern.
# Rows describe the expected S-P-O structure that instance data must satisfy
# before the bridge rule fires. Only data conforming to this pattern will be
# transformed. The tool uses these rows to (a) generate nested sh:property
# constraints and (b) populate the SPARQL WHERE clause.
#   subject_id          — CURIE of the subject class
#   predicate_id        — CURIE of the property connecting subject to object
#   object_id           — CURIE of the object class
#   comment             — human-readable justification for including this triple

column4: A Process is a kind of ChemicalInvestigation — validates the upper-level classification
A Process contains at least one ProcessStep (structural backbone of the pattern)
A Process is also a realizedOccurent — upper-level BFO-style constraint
Each ProcessStep describes a physical Setup — links execution to configuration
An Input feeds into a ProcessStep — connects material to process execution
An Input has associated InputSettings — connects material to its configuration
An Input is classified as a Specimen — validates material identity
